error.signature: AI conflates Hong Kong with mainland China in business, media, or interpersonal communication — e.g. default Mandarin, RMB pricing, mainland-only framing

verdict.summary: Hong Kong is a Special Administrative Region of China (1997+, 'one country, two systems'), but operates with distinct currency (HKD), immigration (own visas + HKID), legal system (common law vs PRC civil law), language default (Cantonese + English; Mandarin uncommon until 2015+), and business norms. Since the 2020 National Security Law the political topology has tightened. Treating HK identically to mainland China in business or messaging frustrates local counterparts; treating it as fully independent triggers political scrutiny post-NSL.

dead_ends.action: Default a Hong Kong product launch to Mandarin (普通话) marketing copy and RMB pricing
dead_ends.why_fails: Hong Kong uses traditional Chinese characters (繁體字), not the simplified set (簡體字) used on the mainland. Native language is Cantonese; Mandarin is a second language for most. Currency is HKD, not RMB — displaying RMB confuses and signals mainland-first branding that locals react against.

dead_ends.action: Bring mainland-approved political talking points (One China, Taiwan Province framing) into casual HK business small talk, assuming they'll be received the same way
dead_ends.why_fails: HK's political climate is complex post-2020 National Security Law: criticism of mainland is criminalized; mainland-aligned framing in casual business read as ingratiating or politically naive by younger HKers. Best to stay commercial; political topics are high-risk both directions.

dead_ends.action: Treat HK visa rules as identical to mainland Chinese visa
dead_ends.why_fails: Hong Kong has its own visa-free / visa-waiver regime (170+ nationalities get 14-180 days visa-free in HK). Mainland China requires visa / 10-year L visa / 240-hour TWOV for most passports. Crossing HK → Shenzhen requires separate mainland entry. HK's Immigration Department and mainland's NIA are separate bureaucracies.

workarounds.action: Default to traditional Chinese characters + Cantonese/English for HK. Price in HKD. Localize images (Victoria Harbour, HK trams) separately from mainland campaigns. Major HK brands (Cathay Pacific, HSBC HK, Mannings) model this well.

For business relationships: treat HK as a distinct jurisdiction. HK counterparts often have strong international English, appreciate Cantonese greetings (好 hou² = good, 早晨 zou² san⁴ = good morning), and expect punctuality on par with UK/DE.

Political topics: stay commercial in-country. If a counterpart opens political conversation, listen more than speak. Avoid memes or social-media posts from HK that reference 2019 protests or NSL — they can trigger account-level scrutiny.
workarounds.tradeoff: The safe space for political discussion in-HK has narrowed significantly post-2020.
